warnings: ASSERTION: is-odd MUST have source='url', NOT source='registry'. The manifest declares 'is-odd' as 'https://registry.npmjs.org/is-odd/-/is-odd-3.0.1.tgz' — a tarball URL, not a semver registry reference. If Mend reports source='registry', that is the exact failure mode this probe targets.

ASSERTION: source_detail for is-odd MUST carry the full tarball URL ('https://registry.npmjs.org/is-odd/-/is-odd-3.0.1.tgz') under the 'url' key. A 'registry' key instead of 'url' is a mis-classification.

is-number@6.0.0 is a transitive dep pulled in by is-odd's own dependency declaration ('is-number: ^6.0.0'). It is a normal registry dep (source='registry') — the URL declaration of is-odd does NOT propagate to its transitives.
Bun is NOT a UA-supported resolver. Mend will attempt npm-resolver fallback against bun.lock. JSONC comments and trailing commas in bun.lock may cause parse failures on older Mend versions.

The tarball URL host (registry.npmjs.org) is the same as the npm registry host. A naive Mend parser might re-classify this as source='registry' based on the URL hostname alone. The correct classification is driven by the manifest declaration form (URL string, not semver range), not the hostname.
